creator: mb@imp.ch
text:
Hi,

I've confirmed it to be a fresh new problem. Kdelibs2.0 compiles fine with
FreeBSD 4.1STABLE, FreeBSD 4.2RELEASE, but not with 4.2 STABLE.

So there was maybe a gcc update or the binutils have been updated since
the 4.2 RELEASE.

I'd like to have it fixed instead of a binary package.

Martin

Martin Blapp, mb@imp.ch
------------------------------------------------
Improware AG, UNIX solution and service provider
Zurlindenstrasse 29, 4133 Pratteln, Switzerland
Phone: +41 79 370 26 05, Fax: +41 61 826 93 01
------------------------------------------------

On Mon, 4 Dec 2000, Vivek Khera wrote:

> >>>>> "m" == mb  <mb@imp.ch> writes:
> 
> >> How-To-Repeat:
> m> The error is repeatable.
> >> Fix:
> m> Not yet known. A kde2 port problem or a g++ problem ?
> 
> Probably neither.  KDE2 (selected components -- I don't need koffice
> and some other toys) compiled just fine on my 4.1.1-STABLE box last
> week; I've since upgraded to 4.2-STABLE but not recompiled KDE.
> 
> I have packages if you want.  Looks like you're croaking in the
> compile of qt22, which I also have a package for.
> 
> --
